title: dryopteris incrassata
intro: Dryopteris incrassata, also known as the thicket fern, is a species of fern that is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. Dryopteris incrassata can be prepared as a decoction or a tea, and it is often used in herbal remedies to support respiratory health and to aid in the treatment of various infections.
